app.title: Sidevers
node.muted: Start the embedded protocol node, then dial a peer.
sides.muted: Each side is an independent identity with its own QUIC endpoint.
peer.muted: Connect to another node by network address.
send.muted: Sent over the active side's open session.
inbox.muted: DMs arriving on this node, newest first.
pair_show.muted: Generate a QR for the active side.
pair_show.uri_label: URI (paste this into the other device)
pair_accept.muted: Paste the URI from the existing device's QR.
onboard.welcome.body: Sidevers is a peer-to-peer protocol for talking to the people you've chosen to talk to, without a server in the middle. Each identity you create here is a self-sovereign "side" — your keys, your data, your reach.
onboard.welcome.next: We'll walk through four steps: pick where Sidevers stores your data, create your first side, back up its key, and then you're done.
onboard.dir.body: Sidevers persists your keys, your inbox, and your peer table here. The default is OS-appropriate. Make sure it's somewhere your OS-level disk encryption covers.
onboard.side.body: A "side" is an identity — call it whatever fits the context. Common labels are work, private, public, close. You can add more sides later from the main UI.
onboard.backup.warn: This is the only copy of your private key. If you lose it and never paired another device, your identity is gone — there's no recovery service. Save the file somewhere safe (a USB stick or encrypted vault, not cloud-sync).
onboard.backup.path_placeholder: where to write the seed file
onboard.done.body: Your side address is below. Hand it to someone you'd like to message, or pair your phone with this device from the main UI to host the same identity in two places.
profile.muted: Per-side profile. Published to peers who fetch it.
contacts.muted: Local-only contacts for the active side.
rail.aria: Hosted sides — pick which one to use
inside.aria: Friends and chats for the active side
inside.empty_friends: No friends yet on this side.
welcome.h: Pick a chat or add a friend
welcome.body: Friends and chats appear on the left for the side you're in. Switch sides on the far-left rail. Click a friend to open a chat.
thread.endpoint_missing: We don't know how to reach this friend yet. Drop their network address below to start the chat.
friend.local_overrides_sub: These only affect this device. Capabilities here override the friend's published profile for what you'll accept.
add_friend.share_sub: Show this code to whoever you want to message. They scan it or paste it into "Add by code" on their end. The code carries your side address + network endpoint — no secrets.
add_friend.share_uri_label: Or paste this code into the other device
add_friend.paste_sub: Paste a friend code (starts with sidevers-contact:1:) or scan the friend's QR. We'll save them as a friend on the active side.
side_settings.profile_sub: Friends see this when they fetch your side. Edits sign + publish from this side; nothing leaves the device until you hit Save.
side_settings.danger_sub: Retiring signs a SideRetirement record. Peers will start treating new envelopes from this side as anomalous. You can still receive replies for a while; you can't undo it.
settings.theme_hint: Stays in sync with the OS by default.
settings.lang_hint: UI strings; Arabic switches to RTL.
settings.advanced_mode_hint: Reveals bech32 addresses, lifecycle states, and the Network / Pair-device / Protocol pages.
settings.advanced_pages_hint: Network, pair another device, raw protocol controls.
settings.backup_sub: Export the active side's seed file. Treat it like a master password — anyone with it can impersonate this side.
settings.about_sub: Sidevers reference client — peer-to-peer messaging without a server.
advanced.pair_sub: Show this QR on your existing device, scan it on a new one to install this side there too. Both devices co-host the same identity. The QR is a 10-minute one-time code.
advanced.pair_uri_label: Or paste this into the other device
advanced.pair_accept_label: Or accept a pairing QR (on the new device)
advanced.dial_sub: Open a session to a peer's network endpoint from the active side. The chat-first UI does this for you when you click a friend; this is an escape hatch when you only have an endpoint, not a friend record.
